response.justification: The candidate is an accomplished senior full-stack/web engineer with breadth across TypeScript, Node.js, DLang, and some C/C++, but the resume shows no experience in the core domain of this role: SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator architecture, GPU or SIMD/MIMD architecture, deep-learning workload analysis, or hardware-software co-design. The closest touchpoints are low-latency DLang services at Sociomantic and C/C++ IDE component work at National Instruments, which are far from silicon performance architecture. While he meets the years-of-experience bar (BS + 15+ years), the domain mismatch at a Principal level is disqualifying.
strengths: Strong low-latency systems experience (async I/O, fibers, caching) handling millions of requests per second
Broad polyglot engineering background with C/C++, DLang, and systems-level exposure
Demonstrated craftsmanship: TDD, open-source authorship, teaching, and long-term project ownership
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, silicon architecture, or hardware performance-modeling experience whatsoever
No AI accelerator, GPU architecture, or deep-learning training/inference optimization background
Career trajectory is web/full-stack product engineering, not principal-level hardware performance architecture

key_factors.factor: No SoC/IP modeling or hardware architecture experience
No AI/deep-learning workload or accelerator expertise
